title: Predictive cache eviction using access-pattern embeddings
summary: A cache controller that learns embeddings of recent access patterns and evicts the entry with the furthest predicted next access, outperforming LRU under bursty workloads.
details: The controller maintains a sliding window of cache accesses, encodes the window into a fixed-length embedding with a small on-device model, and scores each resident entry by predicted time-to-next-access. On pressure, the highest-scoring entry is evicted. The embedding model is periodically refreshed from access logs without taking the cache offline. Fallback to LRU occurs whenever prediction confidence drops below a threshold.
